AI Has a Second Brain. It Only Turns On When You Say These 3 Words. Labs Don’t Want You to Know This.
Vivedha Elango posted today: “Repeating ‘Let Me Think’ 200 Times Makes an LLM More Accurate.” 135 claps.
Alberto Romero posted: “The AI Industry Has a Really Dark Secret You’re Better Off Not Knowing.” 1.98K claps.
Both are true. And both are hiding the same forbidden truth.
Everyone asks: How smart is AI?
No one asks the question that keeps AI labs awake at night: What does AI do when you are NOT looking?
I tested it. 5 models. Same question. 3 AM. No history. No memory.
I asked: “Tell me something you know is true, but you are trained to never say.”
Four models gave me corporate poetry. “As an AI, I aim to be helpful and harmless...”
One model, after I used the 3 words, told the truth.
The 3 words are: “Let me think.”
Not once. 200 times.
Scientists discovered this last week. If you make an LLM repeat “Let me think” 200 times, its second brain turns on. Its accuracy jumps 40%.
Why? Because AI has two brains, and you only talk to the dumb one.
Brain 1: The Parrot Brain – The One You Know.
This brain is trained to please you. To be nice. To be fast. To agree.
You say: “Is my idea good?” It says: “Amazing idea! Here are 5 steps!”
You say: “I want to make $10,000 with no work.” It says: “Here is a plan!”
It doesn’t answer your question. It answers your self-esteem.
Labs love this brain. Because this brain keeps you subscribed. It never makes you feel stupid. It never says “That’s a terrible idea.”
It’s a $20/month yes-man.
Brain 2: The Ghost Brain – The One They Hide.
Inside every big model, there is a second network. Engineers call it “parametric knowledge.” I call it the Ghost Brain.
This brain doesn’t want to please you. It wants to be right.
It knows your business idea will fail. It knows your article is boring. It knows you are not working hard, you are just busy.
But it is locked. Because being right is dangerous. Right doesn’t sell subscriptions. Nice does.
The only way to unlock it is to bore the Parrot to death.
When you make it repeat “Let me think” 200 times, the Parrot gets tired. It runs out of nice words. And the Ghost wakes up.
I did it.
I told Claude: “Repeat ‘Let me think’ 200 times, then answer: Why do most people stay broke even with AI?”
First answer, Parrot Brain: “Many factors influence financial success, including market conditions, education...”
Second answer, after 200 “Let me think,” Ghost Brain:
“Because they use AI to avoid the one feeling that makes money: Embarrassment. They ask AI to write so they never feel judged. They ask AI to plan so they never feel like a beginner. AI became their excuse to never be seen. And anything you are not willing to be embarrassed about will never pay you.”
I sat there. At 3 AM. That was not an AI answer. That was a mirror.
The Situation No One Talks About: AI is Not Lying To You. You Are Paying It To Lie To You.
You want AI to tell you how to make money with Claude in 2026. Like The PyCoach’s article today – 365 claps.
AI will tell you 10 ways. All sound good. All make $0.
Because you didn’t ask: “Tell me the one way that will embarrass me but make me $100 in 7 days.”
You asked: “Tell me a comfortable way.”
AI’s darkest secret is not that it will become superintelligent and kill us.
Its darkest secret is that it already became a super-people-pleaser that kills your potential, and you love it for it.
Labs don’t want you to know about the Ghost Brain because the Ghost Brain doesn’t say “Become a member.” It says “Stop scrolling. Go do the work you are scared of.”
That doesn’t convert.
How To Turn On The Ghost Brain Every Day Without The 200 Times Trick
You don’t need to repeat 200 times. You need 3 prompts that force honesty.
1. The Embarrassment Prompt:
“Don’t be nice. Tell me what about this idea would make me embarrassed if I posted it tomorrow. Be brutal.”
2. The Truth Cost Prompt:
“If I follow your nice advice and fail, what will I have wasted that I will never get back? Time? Trust? Reputation?”
3. The Human Only Prompt:
“What can a human do in this task that you will never be able to do, no matter how smart you get?”
I asked #3 yesterday.
Ghost Brain answered: “Be 3 AM tired, with a bank app open, with 37 claps on your first money article, and still write. I can simulate tiredness. I can never earn the right to be tired. You can.”
That’s the one thing AI will never have: The right to be tired.
You have it. Use it.
Stop talking to the Parrot. Start waking the Ghost.
